issues.display_title: Issue #367: GORM Models Generation from DDL
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 26/06/26

✅ Implementation: GORM Models Generation from DDL

This document tracks the technical implementation for generating the standardized GORM models.

Phase 1: Model Generation (Completed)

Steps Executed:

  1. DDL Parsing:
    • Evaluated 48 DDL files located in docs/plan/migrations/table/.
    • Wrote a local generator script (generate_models.go) to parse table names, columns, SQL types, constraints, and foreign key references.
  2. File Creation:
    • Generated 48 individual model files under src/Models/ (e.g., User.go, Post.go, Comment.go, Affiliate.go).
    • Translated SQL types to Go types (UUID -> uuid.UUID, JSONB -> json.RawMessage, TIMESTAMPTZ -> time.Time).
  3. Compilation Fixes:
    • Resolved Go compilation errors caused by multiple foreign keys pointing to the same table.
    • Updated Followers.go to use User and Follower.
    • Updated UserBlocks.go to use User and BlockedUser.
    • Updated ReportIssueUser.go to use User and ReportedUser.
    • Updated Notifications.go to use User and Sender.
    • Successfully compiled the Models package via go build.

Next Steps

With the foundation of the GORM models now established and successfully compiling, the next logical step (as a separate future task) will be to refactor the Application Layer (Action and Worker packages). This future task will involve replacing manual SQL operations (like tx.Raw and tx.Exec) with native GORM methods (like tx.Create(&Model{}) and .Preload()).

Background

The database schema defines rigorous referential integrity using SQL constraints such as FOREIGN KEY, ON DELETE CASCADE, and ON UPDATE CASCADE. To take full advantage of this within the Go backend (e.g., eager loading, cascading actions, and simpler CRUD operations), the raw DDL scripts must be accurately translated into standard GORM model structs.

Objective

To map all existing database tables (from the DDL migration scripts) to properly configured GORM model structs, ensuring each table has its own dedicated .go file.

Requirements

  1. Model Standardization:
    • Create exactly 1 struct per table.
    • Each model must reside in its own .go file under src/Models/.
    • Explicitly define gorm tags for columns, types, primary keys, and nullability.
    • Implement the TableName() string function for every model to explicitly map it to the database table.
    • Declare SQL relations (Belongs To, Has Many) using GORM foreign key configurations mapped from the DDL CONSTRAINT statements.

Acceptance Criteria

  • 48 DDL scripts are successfully translated into 48 Go model files.
  • go build runs successfully in the Models directory without any duplicate field declarations or syntax errors.
  • GORM models correctly represent the database structure and relationships.

issues.display_title: Issue #369: Refactor Collection Post Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 26/06/26

✅ Implementation: Collection Post Refactoring

This document tracks the execution details of the CollectionPost module refactoring.

Phase 1: CreateCollectionPost.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/CollectionPost/CreateCollectionPost.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Imported panorra-api-new/src/Models and github.com/google/uuid.
    • Removed raw SQL INSERT statements with tx.Raw and RETURNING id.
    • Replaced with Models.CollectionPost struct instantiation and tx.Create().
    • Removed manual string placeholders loop for bulk inserts.
    • Replaced with an array slice []Models.CollectionPostDetail and executed bulk insert via tx.Create(&details).
    • Bug Fix: Explicitly assigned ID: uuid.New() during struct initialization to prevent GORM from inserting zero-value UUIDs (000000...) which caused duplicate key value violates unique constraint errors.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 2: UpdateCollectionPost.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/CollectionPost/UpdateCollectionPost.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Removed risky map[string]interface{} scanning which could panic on NULL database values.
    • Safely fetched data using Config.DB.First(&Models.CollectionPost{}) and checked pointers before assignment.
    • Replaced tx.Exec raw updates with tx.Model().Updates().
    • Replaced raw DELETE FROM with tx.Where().Delete(&Models.CollectionPostDetail{}).
    • Implemented struct array bulk insert for the new details.
    • Bug Fix: Added explicit ID: uuid.New() assignments for newly inserted details to resolve UUID unique constraint violations.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 3: DeleteCollectionPost.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/CollectionPost/DeleteCollectionPost.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Refactored Config.DB.Table().Scan() to use Config.DB.First(&Models.CollectionPost{}).
    • Replaced tx.Exec("DELETE FROM ...") with standard Config.DB.Delete(&Models.CollectionPost{}).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Summary

The CollectionPost module has been fully modernized to use GORM models. The code is now significantly safer, specifically concerning SQL Injection prevention and nil-pointer panic handling when reading nullable fields.

Background

The CollectionPost module currently relies on manual SQL string concatenations and tx.Raw/tx.Exec queries to perform database operations. With the newly generated GORM models, these raw queries can be replaced with safer, more robust ORM methods.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/CollectionPost/ to utilize standard GORM model struct operations (e.g., tx.Create, tx.Updates, tx.Delete).

APIs to Refactor

  • CreateCollectionPost:
    • Replace raw INSERT INTO collection_post ... RETURNING id with tx.Create(&Models.CollectionPost{}).
    • Replace string-join manual bulk insert with GORM array insertion tx.Create(&[]Models.CollectionPostDetail{}).
  • DeleteCollectionPost:
    • Replace Config.DB.Table().Scan() with Config.DB.First(&Models.CollectionPost{}) to safely map pointers and prevent nil panics.
    • Replace tx.Exec("DELETE FROM...") with Config.DB.Delete(&Models.CollectionPost{}).
  • UpdateCollectionPost:
    • Replace manual map casting (collection["thumbnail"].(string)) with typed Models.CollectionPost selection to avoid nil pointer panics.
    • Replace raw UPDATE query with tx.Model().Updates().
    • Replace string-join manual bulk insert for the new collection details with GORM array insertion.
  • UploadThumbnailCollectionPost: (No refactoring needed as it handles MinIO/S3 file uploads without database mutations).

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Proper pointer handling is implemented to prevent panics when reading nullable database columns.
  • Explicitly generate and assign UUIDs using uuid.New() in Go prior to GORM insertion to prevent zero-value UUID constraint violations.
  • go build runs successfully in the module directory.

issues.display_title: Issue #368: Refactor Bookmarks Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 26/06/26

✅ Implementation: Bookmarks Module Refactoring

This document tracks the execution details for the Bookmarks module refactoring.

Phase 1: CreateFolderBookmarks.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Bookmarks/CreateFolderBookmarks.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced Config.DB.Raw("INSERT...") with Models.FolderBookmark{} and Config.DB.Create().
    • Added explicit UUID generation uuid.New().
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 2: AddBookmark.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Bookmarks/AddBookmark.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Removed map[string]interface{} scanning for posts. Used Models.Post instead.
    • Replaced manual bookmark checks with Models.Bookmark and gorm.ErrRecordNotFound.
    • Replaced raw INSERT and DELETE with Config.DB.Create() and Config.DB.Delete().
    • Included uuid.New() logic for generating primary keys.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 3: Update & Delete Folder

  • Target Files: UpdateFolderBookmarks.go, DeleteFolderBookmarks.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • UpdateFolderBookmarks: Replaced Config.DB.Exec("UPDATE ...") with Config.DB.Model(&Models.FolderBookmark{}).Where(...).Update(...).
    • DeleteFolderBookmarks: Replaced Config.DB.Exec("DELETE ...") with Config.DB.Where(...).Delete(&Models.FolderBookmark{}).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 4: PublishFolder.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Bookmarks/PublishFolder.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced struct-scanning table queries with Models.FolderBookmark.
    • Converted table Count checks to use Config.DB.Model().
    • Used .Updates(map[string]interface{}) to dynamically update database fields (both publishing and unpublishing functions).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Background

The Bookmarks module currently handles operations (creating folders, adding bookmarks, publishing folders) using manual SQL queries (tx.Raw, tx.Exec) and maps for retrieving data. Transitioning to GORM models will enforce type safety, avoid nil pointer panics on nullable database columns, and simplify SQL injection prevention.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Bookmarks/ to utilize the existing GORM models (Models.FolderBookmark and Models.Bookmark).

APIs to Refactor

  • CreateFolderBookmarks: Refactor insertion logic to use tx.Create(&Models.FolderBookmark{}).
  • DeleteFolderBookmarks: Refactor deletion query to use Config.DB.Delete().
  • UpdateFolderBookmarks: Refactor update logic to use Config.DB.Model().Updates().
  • AddBookmark: Refactor manual lookup and insert into standard GORM logic.
  • PublishFolder: Refactor logic updating the visibility and states using GORM objects.

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Explicitly generate and assign UUIDs using uuid.New() in Go before insertion to prevent zero-value constraint errors.
  • Untyped map[string]interface{} logic used for reading DB results is replaced with structured GORM model types.
  • The code successfully compiles (go build) without any unused imports.

issues.display_title: Issue #370: Refactor Comments Module to Use GORM Models
issues.main_description_html:
RESULT 26/06/26

✅ Implementation: Comments Module Refactoring

This document tracks the execution details for the Comments module refactoring.

Phase 1: AddComment.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Comments/AddComment.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Replaced manual post lookup query with Config.DB.Preload("User").First(&Models.Post{}).
    • Replaced raw INSERT INTO comment with Config.DB.Create(&Models.Comment{}).
    • Assigned unique UUID using uuid.New() and handled time.Now() parsing natively.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 2: AddReplyComment.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Comments/AddReplyComment.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Separated Post and Comment verification queries to eliminate generic manual joins and use Models.Post and Models.Comment properly.
    • Removed map[string]interface{} logic for extraction.
    • Inserted replies cleanly using Config.DB.Create(&Models.Comment{}).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 3: Edit & Update Comments

  • Target Files: EditComment.go, UpdateReplyComment.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Used Models.Comment alongside First() to perform clean lookup validations.
    • Swapped out raw UPDATE string literals for GORM's .Model(&Models.Comment{}).Where(...).Updates(map[string]interface{}{...}) to dynamically mutate DB values.
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Phase 4: DeleteComment.go

  • Target File: src/Action/V1/Comments/DeleteComment.go
  • Changes Applied:
    • Structured lookups with Models.Comment and Models.Post.
    • Used .Find() on Models.Comment to detect sub-replies that need Elasticsearch cleanup.
    • Eliminated raw DELETE strings for standard ORM .Delete(&Models.Comment{}) and .Delete(&Models.Notifications{}).
  • Status: Completed & Compiled Successfully.

Background

The Comments module currently manages comment creations, replies, edits, and deletions using raw SQL queries and generic map[string]interface{} bindings. Migrating this module to the new GORM models will provide compile-time safety, easier relational parsing, and proper zero-value protections.

Objective

Refactor the API endpoints within src/Action/V1/Comments/ to use structured GORM models (Models.Comment, Models.Post, etc.).

APIs to Refactor

  • AddComment: Refactor raw insertions and map lookups.
  • AddReplyComment: Refactor raw insertions and map lookups.
  • DeleteComment: Refactor DELETE FROM to tx.Delete().
  • EditComment: Refactor UPDATE statements to use tx.Updates().
  • UpdateReplyComment: Refactor UPDATE statements to use tx.Updates().

Acceptance Criteria

  • No raw SQL INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE queries remain in the refactored endpoints.
  • Untyped map[string]interface{} logic used for reading DB results is replaced with structured GORM model types.
  • Explicitly generate and assign UUIDs using uuid.New() in Go before insertion to prevent zero-value constraint errors.
  • The code successfully compiles (go build) in the module directory.
